source: Learning Ansible with Rocky - Ansible Intermediate: Loop management

questions.q: Which keyword iterates a task over a list?
questions.why: loop iterates a task; each value is exposed as item.

questions.q: Inside a loop, which variable holds the current value?
questions.why: At each iteration the value of the list is stored in item.

questions.q: To loop over a dictionary you first convert it. Which Jinja filter does that?
questions.why: | dict2items turns a dict into a list of {key, value} entries you can loop over.

questions.q: After `loop: "{{ users | dict2items }}"`, how do you read each entry's key and value?
questions.why: dict2items exposes item.key (the dictionary key) and item.value (its value).

questions.q: How do you loop over the values of a dict variable 'service' as a list?
questions.why: service.values() piped to the list filter yields a list of the values.

questions.q: You can iterate a registered command's output with...
questions.why: stdout_lines is a list, so it can be looped over directly.
